gaps.issue: Tool call hover/running states still use hardcoded rgba(74, 158, 255, ...) instead of var(--tool-accent-bg)/var(--tool-accent-border). 4 instances in .tool-call:hover and .tool-call.running selectors.
Image hover shadow uses hardcoded rgba(74, 158, 255, 0.2) instead of a tool-accent-derived variable.
Error state background on .message-card uses hardcoded #fef2f2 instead of var(--error-bg).

Code block styling (border, header, line numbers, copy button) uses ~10 hardcoded rgba(255, 255, 255, ...) values. These are code-block-specific and lower priority but break the single-source-of-truth pattern.
Transition durations still inconsistent (0.15s, 0.2s, 0.3s) without named tokens. Lower priority — not addressed in design-4 scope.
No timestamps on messages — conversation lacks temporal anchoring. Requires data model changes, deferred.
gaps.note: Deferred — requires data model changes beyond CSS scope

remainingIssues: 9+ arbitrary font sizes without named scale

afterIteration4.remainingIssues: 4 hardcoded tool-call hover/running colors, code block styling not tokenized, transition durations not standardized, no message timestamps (deferred)

qualityAssessment.improvement: +3.8 points — design system tokens established for colors, typography, and border-radius. 85%+ of hardcoded values replaced.

rationale: Gap score 13.6 is well below the 20 threshold. All three design-4 changes (semantic colors, typography scale, border-radius tiers) are correctly defined in theme.css for both light and dark modes. MessageCard.vue successfully uses the new tokens for the vast majority of selectors. The 4 remaining hardcoded tool-call hover/running colors are a minor miss but don't affect the overall design system quality. The project has progressed from gap score 30.2 (iteration 2) → 17.4 (iteration 3) → 13.6 (iteration 4), a cumulative +16.6 point improvement. The UI now has a proper design token foundation (semantic colors, typography scale, border-radius tiers) that brings it significantly closer to Notion-level design system maturity.
